zh.Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial Am: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas George Jr.於1635年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特時，他的父親Nicholas Lauson George32歲，母親Margaret Saward30歲。他於1648年與Elizabeth Ann James在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1700年7月10日在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas George Jr.于1635年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特时，他的父亲Nicholas Lauson George32岁，母亲Margaret Saward30岁。他于1648年与Elizabeth Ann James在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1700年7月10日在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特。

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, French, and Romanian: from the personal name George, Latin Georgius, Greek Geōrgios, from an adjectival form, geōrgios ‘rustic’, of Greek geōrgos ‘farmer’. This became established as a personal name in classical times through its association with the fashion for pastoral poetry. Its popularity in western Europe increased at the time of the Crusades, which brought greater contact with the Orthodox Church, in which several Christian martyrs and saints of this name are venerated, in particular a saint believed to have been martyred at Nicomedia in AD 303, who, however, is at best a shadowy figure historically. Nevertheless, by the end of the Middle Ages Saint George had become associated with an unhistorical legend of dragon-slaying exploits, which caught the popular imagination throughout Europe, and he came to be considered the patron saint of England among other places. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Georg , Assyrian/Chaldean Giwargis, Gewargis , or Georgis , and Albanian Gjergji , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Georgiadis , Georgopoulos , Hatzigeorgiou ‘George the Pilgrim’, and Papageorgiou , Romanian Georgescu or Gheorghescu, Serbian Djordjevic . The name George is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Geevarghese and Varghese ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: variant of   Georg  .
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name George (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Djordjevic
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Serbian or Croatian Dragan, Bozidar, Branko, Milan, Miodrag, Momcilo, Predrag, Sinisa, Sotir, Borka, Djordje, Dragana, Dunja, Dusan, Mirko, Miroslav, Milutin, Misha, Vladimir.

Serbian and Croatian (Djordjević, standard spelling Đorđević): patronymic from the especially Serbian personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). Đorđević is the fifth most frequent surname in Serbia. Compare Georgevich .

variants.name: Djukic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djukić, standard spelling Đukić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

variants.name: Durko
meanings: Slovak (Ďurko): from the personal name Ďurko, a pet form of Juraj, an equivalent of George . Compare Darko .

variants.name: Gazzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Almando, Attilio, Julio, Salvatore.

Italian: probably from the medieval Greek personal name Gatsos, a pet form of Georgatsos, a derivative of Geōrgios (see George ). Alternatively, a nickname from Sicilian gazzu ‘blue-eyed’ (also ‘short-sighted’), or from Old Italian gazzo ‘deep blue’ (like the feathers of a magpie, Italian gazza). It could also be a habitational name from Gazzo in Padova province, Gazzo Veronese in Verona province, or from a shortened form of Agazzo, from Late Latin Agathius, either a masculine form of Agata or from the analogous ancient Germanic personal name Agathio.

variants.name: Geevarghese
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Geevarghese, a derivative of Greek Geōrgios (see George ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ganta
meanings: Indian (Andhra Pradesh): name borne by a sept of Kamma (agrarian) and Balija (trader) people, from Telugu ganta ‘bell’.
Romanian (Ganţă): from a derivative of the personal name Gană, Gane, Ganu, of Bulgarian origin (a pet form of local names corresponding to George ).

variants.name: Durica
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Moravian) (Ďurica): patronymic from the personal name Ďuro, a vernacular form of local equivalents (Juraj, Jura) of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djuric
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djurić, standard spelling Đurić): patronymic from the personal names Đura and Đuro, short forms of Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). See also Juric , compare Duric .

variants.name: Georg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerd, Kurt, Manfred.
German: from the personal name Georg, an equivalent of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djokic
meanings: Serbian (Djokić, standard spelling Đokić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas George Jr. was born in 1635, in Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, Nicholas Lauson George, was 32 and his mother, Margaret Saward, was 30. He married Elizabeth Ann James in 1648, in Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 July 1700, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas George Jr. est né en 1635, à Lancaster, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Nicholas Lauson George, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret Saward, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Ann James en 1648, à Lancaster, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 juillet 1700, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Lancaster, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas George Jr. è nato il nel 1635 nel Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Nicholas Lauson George, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Saward, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Ann James nel 1648, nel Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 10 luglio 1700, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas George Jr.が1635年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ランカスターで生まれた時，父Nicholas Lauson Georgeは32歳，母Margaret Sawardは30歳であった。1648年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ランカスターでElizabeth Ann Jamesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1700年7月10日に故郷で65歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ランカスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Christ Church, Lancaster, Virginia, Brit: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터 크리스트 c후르치
ko.Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial Am: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터
ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas George Jr.(이)가 1635년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Lauson George(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Saward(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1648년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터에서 Elizabeth Ann James(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1700년 7월 10일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터에 묻혔다.

ru.Christ Church, Lancaster, Virginia, Brit: краист чурч, ланкастир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas George Jr. родился в 1635 г. в местности ланкастир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Nicholas Lauson George было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Saward, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Ann James в 1648 г. в местности ланкастир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 июля 1700 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности ланкастир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.
